response:
SOPHIA HAS TWO SCIENCE TESTS, BIOLOGY AND CHEMISTRY, WITH 25 AND 20 QUESTIONS, RESPECTIVELY. SHE HAS 45 MINUTES TO COMPLETE THE BIOLOGY TEST AND 1 HOUR TO COMPLETE THE CHEMISTRY TEST. IF SOPHIA WANTS TO ALLOCATE THE SAME AMOUNT OF TIME FOR EACH QUESTION, HOW MANY MORE SECONDS CAN SHE SPEND ON EACH QUESTION IN THE CHEMISTRY TEST COMPARED TO THE BIOLOGY TEST?  

[TEXT]  
SOPHIA HAS 45 MINUTES TO COMPLETE THE BIOLOGY TEST, WHICH IS 45 MINUTES EQUAL TO 2700 SECONDS. SHE HAS 1 HOUR TO COMPLETE THE CHEMISTRY TEST, WHICH IS 60 MINUTES EQUAL TO 3600 SECONDS. IF SHE ALLOCATES THE SAME AMOUNT OF TIME FOR EACH QUESTION, THEN SHE CAN SPEND 2700 SECONDS DIVIDED BY 25 QUESTIONS EQUAL TO 108 SECONDS PER QUESTION AND 3600 SECONDS DIVIDED BY 20 QUESTIONS EQUAL TO 180 SECONDS PER QUESTION. THE DIFFERENCE BETWEEN 180 SECONDS AND 108 SECONDS IS 72 SECONDS.  

[PIECE]  
SOPHIA CAN SPEND 72 MORE SECONDS ON EACH QUESTION IN THE CHEMISTRY TEST COMPARED TO THE BIOLOGY TEST.  
SOPHIA HAS 72 MORE SECONDS PER QUESTION IN THE CHEMISTRY TEST THAN IN THE BIOLOGY TEST.
